tool_call.name: think
tool_call.arguments:
{"thought": "The issue is that the raw string literal R"(...)"" contains a double quote character which terminates the raw string literal prematurely. I need to escape the inner quotes differently or use a different approach.

The problem is: R"(^\s*#\s*(\d+)\s+"([^"]*)".*)"

The raw string literal is terminated by the first " inside it. I need to use a different delimiter for the raw string literal.

In C++, raw string literals use the format: R"delimiter(raw_characters)delimiter"

So I can use: R"xxx(pattern)xxx" where xxx is some unique delimiter.

Or I can just avoid using raw string literals and use regular string escaping.

Let me rewrite the regex patterns using a different approach - either use a custom delimiter or just construct the regex differently.", "summary": "Fix raw string literal issue"}
